source_jobs.title: Senior AI/ML Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About

We’re looking for a senior ML/AI Engineer to build Stream, a new conversational interface. As an ML/AI engineer, you will architect, build, and deploy a new interface which spans voice, audio, and agentic control. This role is perfect for someone who cares deeply about real-world ML deployment and low-latency voice interactions.



Responsibilities

 - Design and improve user-facing experiences, including voice, audio, and agentic control.

 - Develop, evaluate, and deploy models spanning cloud models to resource-constrained on-device settings

 - Optimize inference pipelines for latency, reliability, and concurrency

 - Work closely with other ML/AI engineers, infrastructure engineers, designers, and cofounders on existing and future products
   
   

Qualifications

 - 4+ years in artificial intelligence or machine learning applications

 - Experience shipping ML-based products is required

 - Experience in large-language models is required

 - Experience in audio / voice ML is a major plus

 - Comfortable writing production-grade software

 - Passion for human-computer interaction

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage equity

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Engineer, iOS
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About

We’re looking for a senior iOS product engineer to help build Stream, a new conversational interface. The Stream iOS app touches every aspect of our user experience; including device-cloud connectivity, on-device processing, UIUX, and more. You’ll work closely with design, firmware, and backend engineers to create a technically rigorous experience that ultimately feels simple.



Responsibilities

 - Own iOS product delivery—including architecture (Swift), metrics-driven optimization, testing, and releases

 - Work closely with backend services (Websocket) and device integrations (BLE) for low-latency, always-available human-computer interactions

 - Work with product, design, and GTM to ship releases, gather insights, iterate, and improve our experiences for end-users



Qualifications

 - 8+ years building and shipping interactive mobile (iOS) applications

 - Strong proficiency in Swift and complex interactive app development.

 - Deep experience in OS-level integrations such as media control or on-device ML

 - Experience in Python-based servers and the iOS Bluetooth stack are a plus

 - Strong interdisciplinary communication

 - Strong ownership, comfort with ambiguity, and technical output

 - Passion for end-user delight and human-computer interfaces

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage equity

source_jobs.title: Creative Lead, Content
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About the Role

We're looking for an ambitious creator to tell the story of a new kind of computer—one focused on self extension, expanding human expression, and life in the real world. You'll own social, working directly with our founders & Head of Marketing to develop content which communicates Stream, our brand, and the future we're building towards.

We're looking for someone who has built audiences, thinks like a founder, and ships like a creator. There are no playbooks—this role is for someone eager to create things unseen.



Responsibilities

 - Own content strategy and creation across Instagram, YouTube, X, and other platforms.

 - Create short-form video, static posts, and motion graphics—concept to publish.

 - Produce content in partnership with external partners

 - Shoot and edit content with an iPhone-native, high-craft aesthetic.

 - Support brand design as needed including emails, brand collateral.



Qualifications

 - You've built an audience before—for a brand, a product you started, or yourself.

 - You have a portfolio (or feed) that shows taste across video, static, and motion with content you’ve built.

 - You're full-stack: shoot, edit, design, animate. You don't need a team to ship.

 - You think like an owner—identifying opportunities, making bets, moving fast.

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage equity

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing & Community Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About the Role

We're looking for someone to own the engine of our marketing—from nurturing our early community to building systems that turn engagement into insights. You'll be the connective tissue between our customers and our product, ensuring every touchpoint deepens the relationship and surfaces learnings.

This isn't a traditional community manager role. It's part CRM strategist, part community builder, part insights synthesizer. You'll build the infrastructure that makes word-of-mouth happen and turns our most engaged customers into advocates.



Responsibilities

Nurture, Audience & CRM

 - Own email nurture and lifecycle journeys, designing sequences that guide people from waitlist → pre-order → engaged advocate

 - Build and grow owned audiences through content, partnering with brand leads to distribute stories and product thinking across email, site and LinkedIn

 - Track audience engagement and intent signals, using CRM and content performance to identify high-value moments, engaged followers, and potential advocates

 - Build CRM workflows that track customer journeys and surface high-intent moments

Community & Engagement

 - Stand up and manage our community infrastructure

 - Own social media engagement

 - Develop programs that give early customers meaningful ways to participate (beta testing, feedback sessions, referrals)

 - Manage social scheduling and posting in coordination with content strategy

Insights, Analytics & Feedback Loop

 - Synthesize community feedback, comments, and engagement patterns into actionable insights

 - Build and maintain marketing analytics dashboards; report on channel performance, engagement metrics, and conversion trends

 - Monitor the competitive and cultural landscape

 - Surface trends, sentiment shifts, and feature requests to product and leadership

 - Identify customer stories, angles, and use cases that inform marketing and content



Qualifications

 - 3-5 years experience in community, lifecycle marketing, or CRM roles at high-growth technology companies

 - Hands-on experience building community and nurture systems through platforms like Discord, social comment sections, platforms like LinkedIn, email/CRM tools, and analytics platforms

 - Systems executor mindset: you build repeatable processes that create operational velocity

 - Insights-aware: you notice patterns in engagement, comments, and cultural shifts, and know how to act on them

 - Strong collaborative skills: This role will work closely with brand/strategy leadership and content production partners

 - Strong writing skills: you can represent the brand in customer-facing communications

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and moving fast at an early-stage company

 - Experience building from the ground up—you've started systems, not just maintained them

 - Experience at a hardware company or technology start up is preferred, experience with consumer brands is a plus

 - Background in user research or product feedback synthesis is a plus

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage equity

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About

We’re looking for an experienced infrastructure engineer to help build Stream, a new conversational interface. You will be responsible for developing a highly scalable real-time production service with various steps of ML and data processing. You’ll work closely with other mobile and firmware engineers to deliver the foundations for new human-computer interactions.



Responsibilities

 - Architect a high-uptime, low-maintenance, and reliable distributed inference system

 - Lead observability and oncall

 - Achieve very low latency & jitter with attention to P99 performance

 - Develop a safe rollout to production process in partnership with ML, SW, and mobile engineers

 - Implement data pipelines for user-facing features and internal tooling



Qualifications

 - Proficient in Python and Javascript

 - Experience in shipping consumer facing interactive real-time services

 - 2+ years owning a live production system in a small team, for large N of users

 - 2+ years working in a big infra teams (Google / Meta / etc)

 - Technical leadership for building a robust process with the team

 - Strong ownership on healthy live service

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage equity

source_jobs.title: Lead Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



An Open Letter: Design @ Sandbar https://x.com/minafahmi/status/2034121829942444379



About

We’re looking for a Lead Product Designer for Stream, a new conversational interface. This role touches every aspect of our end-user experience—including UI/UX, multimodal interactions, agentic control, information representation, personality, and more. You’ll work closely with software engineers, machine learning engineers, and founders to lead the design of a new computer.

This role is perfect for someone with wide vision and minute attention to detail, who is humanistic and technical, who is experienced and yet continually ready to start over.



Responsibilities

 - Own end-to-end product design spanning visual, AI, and interaction design

 - Ideate, prototype, and evaluate novel software features

 - Work closely with engineers and product to scope and implement new experiences



Qualifications

 - Experience developing design systems spanning multiple platforms

 - Experience shipping iOS-based apps is required

 - Demonstrated passion and enthusiasm for large language models

 - Experience with novel form factors, inputs, audio, or vision are a plus

 - Experience in non-software design disciplines such as architecture or industrial design is a plus

 - Portfolio of prior work is required

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage equity

source_jobs.title: Operations Program Manager, Hardware
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About

We are seeking a hands-on Operations Program Manager to own end-to-end execution across our Operations function from late DVT through PVT, MP ramp, and sustaining production. This role is responsible for ensuring that engineering outputs transition cleanly into scalable, repeatable operations. This role sits at the center of manufacturing readiness, supply chain execution, logistics coordination, and quality ramp, working closely with our Head of Operations and cross-functional partners.



Responsibilities

Operations Execution

 - Own operations execution plans aligned to the TPM-led master schedule.

 - Translate engineering milestones into concrete manufacturing, supply chain, logistics, and quality deliverables.

 - Drive closure of operational dependencies required to support PVT, MP ramp, and sustaining builds.

 - Manage operational impacts of engineering changes in partnership with TPM.

Manufacturing Ramp

 - Lead factory readiness activities including line setup, capacity planning, pilot builds, and ramp execution.

 - Ensure readiness of BOMs, tooling, test flows, packaging, OQC plans, and work instructions.

 - Partner with Quality and CM teams to track yield and defect trends through ramp.

 - Support daily and weekly factory execution reviews as needed.

Supply Chain & Logistics

 - Coordinate with the Supply Chain Lead on material readiness, long-lead item tracking, risk buys, and supplier issue resolution.

 - Work with the Logistics PM to align inbound materials, outbound shipments, regional allocations, and delivery commitments to production plans.

 - Ensure operational plans are executable against real-world supply and logistics constraints.

Field Quality & Sustaining Operations

 - Own field quality execution: tracking and triage of customer-reported issues, RMAs, and early failure trends.

 - Drive EFFA and Pareto analysis across factory defects, OQC failures, and field returns to focus teams on highest-impact issues.

 - Coordinate containment, root cause analysis, and corrective actions (8D / CAPA) for systemic issues.

 - Establish feedback loops between Customer Support, CM, Quality, and Supply Chain so field learnings are reflected in production.

 - Own sustaining readiness including ECO rollouts, rework planning, inventory disposition, and yield stability post-ramp.

Dashboards, Metrics & Cadence

 - Own operational dashboards (yield, defect, OQC, field-quality Pareto) and the Operations RAID log; ensure data accuracy with CM, OQC, and Quality partners.

 - Proactively surface execution gaps with clear options and tradeoffs.

 - Run core Ops execution meetings and provide concise, decision-oriented updates to leadership.

 - Act as single point of accountability for day-to-day operational execution health.
   
   

Qualifications

 - 7+ years experience in Operations Program Management, Manufacturing Program Management, or similar roles in hardware products.

 - Direct experience supporting EVT → DVT → PVT → MP transitions.

 - Strong understanding of manufacturing, supply chain, logistics, and quality workflows.

 - Proven ability to manage external partners and contract manufacturers.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication; able to drive clarity in ambiguous, fast-moving environments.

 - Comfortable operating in a startup or early-scale environments

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage

source_jobs.title: Lab Technician
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About

We’re seeking a senior Hardware Lab Technician to support the development of new wearable consumer electronics products. In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with our Electrical, Mechanical, Firmware, and Quality engineers to ensure efficient lab operations, prototype testing, and rapid iteration in a fast-paced product development environment. The ideal candidate thrives in a dynamic startup setting, maintains rigorous lab standards, and has a proven track record of filtering through complex hardware challenges to deliver reliable results.



Responsibilities

 - Perform advanced electrical and mechanical testing, debugging, and rework on prototypes, including failure analysis, root cause identification, and data-driven reporting to engineering teams.

 - Build and assemble electronic devices, mechanical fixtures, and test setups; develop and execute automated test scripts using tools like LabVIEW or Python for efficient data collection and validation.

 - Order, maintain, calibrate, and troubleshoot lab equipment, tools, and consumables, ensuring compliance with safety protocols (e.g., ESD, chemical handling) and maintaining a clean, organized, and secure lab space.

 - Manage the full lifecycle of prototype hardware, including receiving, inventory tracking, secure storage, distribution, and disposal, while optimizing for efficiency in high-volume prototyping cycles.

 - Own and optimize hardware-specific IT systems, including network configurations, data logging setups, and integration with cloud-based tools for remote access and collaboration.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with engineers to prototype new circuits, resolve on-site issues, and support remote troubleshooting; train junior team members or lab users on equipment best practices and safety.

 - Analyze test data using statistical methods to identify defects, trends, and improvements; prepare detailed reports and visualizations to inform product decisions.



Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience as a lab technician in a hardware product development environment, preferably in consumer electronics, wearables, or high-tech R&D labs

 - Proven expertise in soldering and reworking fine-pitch components (e.g., 0201 or smaller SMT, through-hole, and BGA), with hands-on experience in PCB assembly, modification, and validation.

 - Ability to read and interpret complex electrical schematics, board layouts (using tools like Allegro or similar CAD viewers), and perform signal integrity analysis.

 - Proficiency with a wide range of electrical test equipment, including oscilloscopes, spectrum analyzers, multimeters, power supplies, data loggers, e-loads, and logic analyzers.

 - Strong knowledge of operating systems (e.g., Windows, macOS, Linux) and basic networking (e.g., switches, routers, remote desktop); experience with scripting for automation (e.g., Python, Bash) to streamline testing workflows.

 - Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related technical field; relevant certifications (e.g., IPC-A-610 for soldering, CompTIA A+ for hardware, or lab safety training) are highly preferred.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills with a detail-oriented approach; ability to multitask in a collaborative team environment, communicate technical findings clearly, and document processes meticulously.

 - Commitment to health, safety, and environmental standards; experience maintaining lab compl

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About

We’re looking for an experienced software product engineer to help build Stream, a new conversational interface. We aspire to make technology as easy as thinking—fast, intuitive, and always available. This role will extend Stream to new surfaces and use cases, and is perfect for someone who sits at the intersection of software engineering, interaction design, and consumer products.



Responsibilities

 - Extend Stream into novel desktop, web, and other surfaces—owning the full stack from UI to backend

 - Design and build interaction patterns for novel input-output paradigms

 - Develop new features and integrations end-to-end, including backend services where needed

 - Collaborate with iOS, firmware, and backend engineers to maintain a seamless cross-platform experience

 - Work with product, design, and GTM to ship releases, gather insights, and iterate



Qualifications

 - 3+ years building and shipping consumer-facing software products

 - Strong proficiency in modern web technologies (TypeScript, React or similar) and desktop frameworks (Swift, Electron)

 - Comfort working across the stack—backend services, APIs, and frontend

 - Track record of building 0-to-1 products or features with ambiguity

 - Excellent attention to interaction details

 - Experience with real-time or interactive applications is a plus

 - Experience with audio/media APIs is a plus

 - Strong interdisciplinary communication

 - Strong ownership, comfort with ambiguity, and high technical output

 - Passion for human-computer interfaces

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage equity

source_jobs.title: Head of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About

As Head of Engineering, you’ll lead our software and machine learning teams in building a new interface that makes all of computing feel as simple as thinking. You’ll guide and grow our mobile, infrastructure, backend, and AI/ML teams and work closely alongside our product, hardware, and firmware teams. You’ll play a significant role in shipping our first product, Stream, as well as shaping our future product, technical, and team direction.

This role is perfect for an experienced engineering leader who cares deeply about team growth and culture, and who wants to build tools that augment individuals in an agentic world.



Responsibilities

 - Lead our software and ML engineering teams across mobile, backend, infrastructure, and AI/ML

 - Drive product delivery—balancing shipping, quality, and velocity

 - Define engineering requirements and goals and facilitate sprints

 - Provide architectural guidance, conduct code reviews, and mentor engineers

 - Collaborate closely with hardware, firmware, and product to ship cohesive, full-stack products

 - Shape technical and product direction—identifying where to invest and what to build next



Qualifications

 - 8+ years in software engineering or machine learning with 3+ years leading engineering teams across multiple disciplines

 - Experience shipping consumer-facing software / ML products

 - Strong technical breadth across mobile, backend, and ML—able to go deep when needed and guide architecture across the stack

 - Track record of building high-performing teams in fast-moving, ambiguous environments

 - Passion for human-computer interaction

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage equity

source_jobs.title: Senior Firmware Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Sandbar

Sandbar is an interface company in New York City. We aim to augment individuals so we can each think, act, and move more freely. Our team has built SW, ML, and HW products across Meta, CTRL-labs, Google, Apple, Fitbit, Peloton, and Equinox.



Our first product, Stream, is a self extension—a private voice ring and conversational interface. Stream has been featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, & Wired, and begins shipping in Summer '26.



Join us in creating technology that extends human thinking.



About

As a Senior Firmware Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, developing, testing, and implementing firmware for embedded systems.

This role requires a deep understanding of both hardware and software, and the ability to work closely with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality products.

You will be involved in all stages of firmware development from concept to production.



Responsibilities

 - Design and develop firmware for embedded systems

 - Collaborate with hardware engineers to define and implement firmware requirements

 - Perform system integration tests to ensure firmware functionality

 - Debug and troubleshoot firmware and hardware issues

 - Document firmware design and development processes

 - Collaborate with manufacturing partners
   
   

Qualifications

 - Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or related field

 - Minimum of 8+ years of experience in firmware development

 - Strong proficiency in C/C++ programming languages.

 - Experience with real-time operating systems (RTOS).

 - Familiarity with hardware design and documentation.

 - Proven ability to debug and troubleshoot complex systems.



Skills

 - C/C+, Python (debug tools)

 - RTOS

 - Embedded Systems, Hardware Interfacing, Debugging Tools

 - Version Control Systems (e.g., Git)

 - Microcontrollers

 - Communication Protocols (e.g., UART, SPI, I2C)

 - BLE protocol

 - Experience with capacitive sensors and/or sensor fusion is a plus
   
   

FTE Benefits

 - Health, vision, and dental benefits

 - Company-sponsored 401(k)

 - Unlimited PTO and sick time

 - Early stage equity

notes: Highly technical roles, strong focus on AI/ML and software engineering
